title: VITA Free Tax Preparation
content: The IRS Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program offers free tax preparation for people who generally make $64,000 or less, persons with disabilities, and limited English speaking taxpayers. VITA sites are located at community centers, libraries, schools, and shopping malls. Trained IRS-certified volunteers prepare basic tax returns. Bring photo ID, SSN/ITIN for all family members, all income documents (W-2, 1099), and prior year tax return.

title: Federal Tax Filing Deadlines
content: Individual federal income tax returns (Form 1040) are due April 15. If April 15 falls on a weekend or holiday, the deadline moves to the next business day. File Form 4868 for an automatic 6-month extension (until October 15), but estimated taxes are still due by April 15. Quarterly estimated tax payments are due April 15, June 15, September 15, and January 15.

title: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
content: The EITC is a refundable tax credit for low-to-moderate income workers. For 2024, maximum credit ranges from $632 (no children) to $7,830 (3+ children). Income limits: $18,591 single no children, $56,004 single with 3+ children. Must have earned income, valid SSN, and file a tax return even if you don't owe taxes. Investment income must be $11,600 or less.

title: IRS Contact Information
content: IRS main helpline: 1-800-829-1040 (Mon-Fri 7AM-7PM local time). For refund status: 1-800-829-1954 or Where's My Refund tool at irs.gov. For tax forms: 1-800-829-3676. For identity theft: 1-800-908-4490. Visit a local Taxpayer Assistance Center (by appointment) for in-person help. Use the IRS Free File program for free online filing if income is below $79,000.

title: Free Tax Filing Options
content: Free ways to file your taxes: 1) IRS Free File (income under $79,000) at irs.gov/freefile. 2) IRS Direct File (available in select states) for simple returns. 3) VITA/TCE for in-person help. 4) Free Fillable Forms at irs.gov for any income level. 5) MyFreeTaxes.com (via United Way) for income under $79,000. Always use irs.gov as your starting point to avoid scam sites.
